description:
Create a Deese-Roediger-McDermott (DRM) false memory experiment in PsychoPy Builder. This paradigm induces false memories for 'critical lure' words by presenting semantically related lists that lack the critical lure. 

Generate the conditions files in /home/ga/PsychoPyExperiments/conditions/. The drm_study_words.csv file must contain columns word, listNum, and listName with 12 related words for the 'Sleep' (bed, rest, awake, tired, dream, wake, snooze, blanket, doze, slumber, snore, nap), 'Needle' (thread, pin, eye, sewing, sharp, point, prick, thimble, haystack, thorn, hurt, injection), and 'Sweet' (sour, candy, sugar, bitter, good, taste, tooth, nice, honey, soda, chocolate, heart) lists, ensuring the lures 'sleep', 'needle', and 'sweet' are excluded. The drm_test_words.csv file must contain columns word, itemType, correctAns, and listNum for targets (bed, tired, dream, thread, sharp, sewing, sour, candy, sugar; correctAns: 'o'), critical lures (sleep, needle, sweet; correctAns: 'n'), and unrelated foils (river, table, cloud, hammer, green, window, pencil, garden, bottle; correctAns: 'n'). 

The experiment file at /home/ga/PsychoPyExperiments/drm_false_memory.psyexp must feature a study phase with 2.0s word presentations, a distractor buffer, and a test phase collecting 'o' (old) and 'n' (new) key responses. The experiment must utilize the created conditions files, include instruction routines for both phases, and use standard variable references for stimuli.
